text.title: Modular Monolith Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: A 'utils' / 'common' module accumulates code from every other module.
antipattern.fix: Rename / split into context-named modules; auto-block PRs that grow `utils/` by > N lines.

antipattern.symptom: Module B imports `from a.domain.models import X` directly.
antipattern.root-cause: Public API not enforced; r2 violated.
antipattern.fix: Add import-linter independence contract; CI rejects the import.

antipattern.symptom: ORM query joins orders + payments tables in a single SELECT.
antipattern.fix: Move payments to its own schema; cross-context reads via API call, not JOIN; database-level grants block cross-schema JOINs from app code.

antipattern.symptom: import-linter contract failing for weeks; CI ignored with --allow-failure.
antipattern.root-cause: r4 conviction softens under deadline pressure.
antipattern.fix: Treat boundary violations as build-breakers; back-out commits introducing them rather than disabling the rule.
